# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build an Elasticsearch snapshot configuration for automated backups. Configure snapshot repository to S3 or file share for the operations team. Implement snapshot creation and restoration functionality.
# Category: datastore_security

# Create S3 Repository
# Requires the 'repository-s3' plugin to be installed on all master and data nodes.
# AWS credentials should be configured in elasticsearch.yml (s3.client.default.access_key, s3.client.default.secret_key)
# or via IAM roles for EC2 instances.
#
# PUT _snapshot/my_s3_repository
---
type: s3
settings:
  bucket: your-s3-bucket-name
  region: us-east-1 # e.g., us-east-1, eu-west-1
  base_path: snapshots/my_cluster # Optional: A path within the bucket
  compress: true # Optional: Compress snapshot data (default: true)
  server_side_encryption: true # Optional: Enable S3 server-side encryption (default: false)
  storage_class: standard # Optional: S3 storage class (e.g., standard, infrequent_access, glacier)

# Create File System Repository
# The 'location' path must be registered in the 'path.repo' setting in elasticsearch.yml
# on all master and data nodes. This path must be a shared file system accessible by all nodes.
#
# PUT _snapshot/my_fs_repository
---
type: fs
settings:
  location: /mnt/backups/elasticsearch # This path must be configured in path.repo in elasticsearch.yml
  compress: true # Optional: Compress snapshot data (default: true)
  max_restore_bytes_per_sec: 40mb # Optional: Throttles the restore operation (default: 40mb)
  max_snapshot_bytes_per_sec: 40mb # Optional: Throttles the snapshot operation (default: 40mb)

# Create a Snapshot
# This will create a snapshot named 'snapshot_20231027' in 'my_s3_repository'
#
# PUT _snapshot/my_s3_repository/snapshot_20231027
---
indices: "index1,index2,logstash-*" # Comma-separated list of indices to snapshot. Use "*" for all indices.
ignore_unavailable: false # If true, ignore unavailable indices during snapshot.
include_global_state: true # Include cluster state (templates, settings, etc.) in the snapshot.
partial: false # Allow partial snapshots if some shards are unavailable.
metadata: # Optional: Custom metadata for the snapshot
  version: 1.0
  taken_by: operations_team
  reason: daily_backup

# Restore a Snapshot (Full Cluster)
# This will restore the entire cluster state and all indices from 'snapshot_20231027'.
# All existing indices will be closed and deleted before restoration.
#
# POST _snapshot/my_s3_repository/snapshot_20231027/_restore
---
indices: "*" # Restore all indices from the snapshot
include_global_state: true # Restore cluster global state (templates, settings, etc.)
partial: false # Allow partial restore if some shards are unavailable
rename_pattern: "(.+)" # Optional: Pattern to rename indices during restore
rename_replacement: "restored_$1" # Optional: Replacement string for renaming indices

# Restore a Snapshot (Specific Indices with Renaming)
# This will restore only 'index1' and 'logstash-2023-10-26' from 'snapshot_20231027',
# renaming them to 'restored_index1' and 'restored_logstash-2023-10-26'.
#
# POST _snapshot/my_s3_repository/snapshot_20231027/_restore
---
indices: "index1,logstash-2023-10-26" # Specific indices to restore
include_global_state: false # Do not restore cluster global state
partial: false
rename_pattern: "(.+)" # Pattern to match index names
rename_replacement: "restored_$1" # Prefix restored indices with "restored_"
wait_for_completion: true # Optional: Block until the restore operation completes (default: false)
